flashcards.back: A data-driven inference method that starts from known facts and applies rules to derive new conclusions.

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between forward and backward chaining?
flashcards.back: Forward chaining is data-driven (starts from facts), while backward chaining is goal-driven (starts from query).
Logical clauses with at most one positive literal, used in forward chaining systems.
The process of finding a substitution that makes two logical expressions identical.

flashcards.front: Name one optimization technique for forward chaining.
flashcards.back: The RETE algorithm for efficient pattern matching.
